fields.description:
## Why
Every prompt/detector change in this epic must be measured, or we repeat dso's mistake of uncalibrated
drift. This is the gating foundation: no prompt story merges without a passing eval.

## What
FIRST extend the eval harness to support code-review prompts, THEN author specs:
- Harness prerequisite (grounded gap from plan review): eval_solver.run_case() has NO execution arm for
  code-review prompt ids (code-review-base, code-review-verify, overlay ids), and eval_scorers.py
  REGISTRY has no scorer validating a code_review_* output. Add a run_case arm that executes these
  prompts and register code-review gating scorers BEFORE specs can run.
- Then build eval specs for base/overlays/verify with two families seeded from dso's corpus:
  NO-FIRE (must not flag): sourced-bash functions, subdirectory scripts, list-form subprocess, Rule-5
  structural greps, moved-code renames with callers updated, already-public config reads, valid
  observable-postcondition tests.
  SEEDED-DEFECT recall (must flag): tautological test, dangling deletion, asymmetric signature change,
  hallucinated API, VCS conflict marker, ticket scope drift.
- A deterministic scorer GATES; an llm-judge only REPORTS.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py (new code-review arm), eval_scorers.py (new scorers),
src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (specs + fixtures). No gate behavior change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] eval_solver.run_case executes code-review prompt ids (base, verify, overlays); >=1 code_review_*
      gating scorer registered in eval_scorers.py.
- [ ] Eval specs exist for base, verify, and at least the tests + security overlays.
- [ ] No-fire and recall case families seeded from the dso incidents above.
- [ ] The nit-framing experiment is encoded as a committed eval case (fixture + scorer assertion), not
      merely referenced; its no-fire/recall expectations pass on the current gate as baseline.
